title: 10 best herbal decoctions for overeating
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for overeating are natural remedies made by steeping herbs in hot water to create a soothing drink that helps alleviate symptoms associated with overindulgence. These concoctions work by calming digestive discomfort, reducing bloating and cramps, and regulating appetite. Examples of effective herbal decoctions include peppermint, ginger, and dandelion root tea, which can ease nausea and flatulence. By incorporating these teas into daily routines, individuals can improve digestion, boost energy levels, and regain control over their eating habits, leading to a more balanced and healthier lifestyle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guduchi decoctions helps with overeating because it has a natural ability to reduce cravings for unhealthy foods. The herb's bitter taste triggers the release of digestive enzymes, which aids in efficient nutrient absorption and satiety. Additionally, guduchi is known to normalize blood sugar levels, reducing the likelihood of mid-day cravings. As the body becomes more attuned to its natural hunger cues, individuals are less likely to overindulge in snacks or meals, promoting a balanced relationship with food and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Guduchi decoction helps to balance the body's stress response, which can trigger overeating habits by reducing cortisol levels and promoting a sense of calm.
Appetite-suppressing property: The decoction contains compounds that can help reduce appetite and improve satiety by slowing down gastric emptying and increasing feelings of fullness.
Digestive-friendly property: Guduchi decoction soothes the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort, which can lead to overeating as a way to alleviate symptoms like bloating or cramps.
remedy_constituents: Gymnemic acid: This triterpenoid saponin helps with overeating by inhibiting the absorption of glucose and reducing sweet taste perception, thereby reducing sugar cravings and appetite.
Gymnenic acids and flavonoids: These compounds help by reducing inflammation and improving insulin sensitivity, which can contribute to overeating and weight gain.

Alkaloids (gymnocin, gymnorin): These compounds help by reducing the release of ghrelin, a hormone that stimulates appetite, and increasing the release of cholecystokinin, a hormone that suppresses appetite, thereby reducing hunger and overeating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of gymnemic acid, which is believed to help reduce sugar cravings and regulate appetite.
Roots: Utilized for their ability to balance blood sugar levels and potentially aid in weight management by reducing overeating.
Seeds: Employed for their nutritional and medicinal properties, including potential weight management benefits due to the presence of various bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Collect 30-60 grams of dried gymnema sylvestre leaves and stems from a reliable source.
Crush the gymnema sylvestre leaves and stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powder with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer the decoction for 5-10 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with overeating because it aids in digestion, reducing bloating and discomfort that often follows overindulgence. The natural oils in fennel seeds have a calming effect on the digestive system, easing symptoms of indigestion and discomfort caused by consuming large portions or rich foods. This allows the body to break down nutrients more efficiently, alleviating feelings of fullness and sluggishness, making it easier to get back on track with healthy eating habits.
remedy_properties: Digestive Carminative Properties: Fennel decoction helps alleviate bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with overeating by relieving gas and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract. This property makes it an effective natural remedy for alleviating post-meal discomfort.

Appetite Suppressant Properties: The volatile oils present in fennel decoction have been shown to suppress appetite, reducing cravings for unhealthy snacks and helping regulate food intake. This property makes it a useful tool for managing overeating habits.

Antioxidant Properties: Fennel decoction contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. By reducing oxidative stress, fennel decoction may help mitigate some of the negative effects associated with overeating.
remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: Foeniculin, a sesquiterpene, is believed to have a regulatory effect on appetite and satiety, helping to curb overeating by reducing hunger pangs.

Anethole: Anethole, a phenylpropene, has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce overeating by alleviating emotional eating triggered by stress and inflammation.
Daucosterol: Daucosterol, a triterpene saponin, may aid in weight management by regulating lipid metabolism and reducing fat absorption, helping to prevent overeating and promote healthy weight loss.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Dried Foeniculum vulgare leaves are used to make decoctions for overeating due to their carminative properties, helping to ease digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
Seeds: The seeds of Foeniculum vulgare are used in decoctions for overeating as they contain oils with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the digestive system.
Stems: The stems of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make decoctions for overeating due to their ability to aid in the relaxation of the digestive muscles, helping to prevent symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried foeniculum vulgare and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush.
Combine the dried foeniculum vulgare with 1 quart of cold water in a large saucepan.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the mixture for 20-25 minutes or until the liquid is reduced.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle decoctions helps with overeating because it stimulates the production of bile, which aids in digestion and absorption of nutrients. This can help reduce feelings of bloating and discomfort that often accompany overindulgence, making it easier to stop overeating. Additionally, milk thistle has anti-inflammatory properties that may soothe digestive issues, such as cramps and nausea, associated with consuming large amounts of food. By promoting a healthy digestive system, milk thistle decoctions can help individuals develop healthier eating habits and reduce the likelihood of overeating.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to overeating and food cravings. This reduction in inflammation can help regulate appetite and improve overall digestive health.

Saponins: The saponin content of milk thistle decoctions has been demonstrated to have choleretic properties, meaning it helps stimulate bile production in the liver and gallbladder. This increased bile flow can aid in the digestion and absorption of nutrients, reducing cravings for unhealthy or excessive food consumption.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in milk thistle decoctions have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety-related overeating behaviors by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the brain.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds, particularly silymarin, help alleviate symptoms of overeating by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, supporting its overall health and function.

Sesquiterpene lactones: These bioactive compounds, including dehydrocostus lactone, have been shown to inhibit the growth of microorganisms in the gut, helping to regulate digestion and prevent conditions that can lead to overeating.

Phenolic acids: These compounds, including caffeic acid, have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the negative effects of overeating on the body, including reduced inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make decoctions due to their milky sap, which is believed to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of overeating.
Leaves: Used for decoctions as they contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with overeating.
Stems: Used in decoctions as they contain silymarin, a compound that may help protect the liver and aid in digestion after overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 cups of dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves in a clean environment.
Combine the dried silybum marianum with 4 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 10 to 15 minutes or until it reaches a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 to 30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with overeating because they promote digestion and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The plant's natural diuretic properties also help to alleviate water retention, which can contribute to feelings of bloating and discomfort after eating. Additionally, dandelion's bitter flavor stimulates saliva production, aiding in the breakdown of food and reducing cravings for unhealthy snacks. By supporting healthy digestion and alleviating uncomfortable symptoms, herbal dandelion decoctions can help individuals develop a healthier relationship with food and reduce overeating habits.
remedy_properties: Choleretic properties: Dandelion decoction stimulates the release of bile from the liver, which helps to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating associated with overeating.
Diuretic properties: The diuretic effects of dandelion decoction can help to reduce water retention, alleviate bloating, and support weight loss by flushing out excess fluids in the body.
Laxative properties: Dandelion's gentle laxative action can help regulate bowel movements, prevent constipation, and reduce symptoms of discomfort and indigestion that may contribute to overeating.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin helps with overeating by acting as a prebiotic, promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria and improving digestion, reducing the likelihood of overeating due to poor gut health.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol has been found to have appetite-suppressing properties, helping to reduce overeating by influencing the brain's appetite centers and improving satiety.

Phenolic acids (such as chlorogenic acid): Phenolic acids in Dandelion decoctions may help with overeating by inhibiting the breakdown of carbohydrates into glucose, which can lead to overeating due to increased insulin resistance and subsequent cravings for more carbohydrates.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are commonly used to make decoctions for overeating due to their diuretic properties, which help in flushing out excess fluids and toxins.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make decoctions for overeating as they contain inulin, a natural fiber that helps in reducing bloating and promoting digestion.
Seeds: Seeds are used to make decoctions for overeating as they contain inulin and other compounds that aid in reducing appetite and promoting weight loss.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves from a trusted source.
Weigh the dried plant material and add it to a medium-sized pot.
Boil 1 liter of water for 10-15 minutes to create the base of the decoction.
Combine the boiled water with the dried taraxacum officinale and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo decoctions helps with overeating because it targets the root cause of excessive food consumption - emotional eating. The herb's calming properties soothe the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety triggers that often drive people to overindulge in food. Additionally, wild indigo's natural antispasmodic effects can help ease digestive discomfort, allowing individuals to better recognize their body's hunger and fullness cues, leading to healthier eating habits and reduced overeating.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant Properties: Wild indigo's decoction stimulates digestion, which can help alleviate feelings of bloating and discomfort associated with overeating, making it easier to recover from indulgence.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of wild indigo may help reduce the negative impact of overeating on the body by combating oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to digestive issues and weight gain.

Blood Sugar Regulation Properties: Wild indigo has been traditionally used to regulate blood sugar levels, which can be beneficial for individuals who overeat due to emotional or psychological triggers related to blood sugar fluctuations.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline alkaloids: These compounds may help with overeating by reducing appetite and improving digestion, potentially due to their ability to modulate the gut-brain axis and influence satiety signals.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds may help with overeating by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to overeating behaviors and metabolic dysregulation.
Saponins: These glycosides may help with overeating by reducing calorie intake and improving lipid metabolism, potentially due to their ability to inhibit fat absorption and modulate gut microbiota.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which help in digestion and reducing symptoms of indigestion.
Leaves: Utilized for their ability to stimulate digestive enzymes, aiding in the breakdown of food and alleviating discomfort.
Seeds: Employed for their purported diuretic properties, which help in flushing out excess water and reducing bloating associated with overeating.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part dried baptisia tinctoria root and 2 parts fresh or dried leafy herb material in a clean environment.
Combine the gathered herb material with 1 quart of water in a large pot to create a decoction.
Boil the mixture for 10 to 30 minutes to extract the desired medicinal properties from the herb material.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature for 30 minutes before consumption or further use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with overeating because it stimulates the stomach to produce more digestive enzymes, making it easier for the body to break down and absorb nutrients. Additionally, licorice root has a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort that can often trigger overeating habits. By supporting digestion and alleviating discomfort, herbal licorice decoctions can help individuals develop healthier eating habits and reduce their likelihood of overindulgence.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has been shown to suppress appetite and reduce food intake by activating the body's natural satiety hormones, leading to weight loss.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers of overeating, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.

Saponins: Licorice root saponins have been found to regulate blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity, which can reduce cravings for sugary or high-carb foods that often contribute to overeating.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with overeating by inhibiting the activity of 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, an enzyme that contributes to increased appetite and weight gain.
Licene: It aids in reducing overeating by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which may help alleviate stress and anxiety-related eating behaviors.
Flavonoids (mainly isoflavones): They may help reduce overeating by modulating the gut microbiota and improving insulin sensitivity, leading to a reduction in appetite and body weight.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make decoctions as they contain glycyrrhizin, which helps to alleviate symptoms of overeating by reducing water retention and aiding digestion.
Leaves: The leaves are used in decoctions due to their ability to help alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating associated with overeating.
Barks: The barks are used to make decoctions as they contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues caused by overeating.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-20 grams of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Crush the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase their surface area.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of the powder with 1 liter of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Boil the mixture for 10-15 minutes then reduce the heat to a simmer for an additional 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove any remaining solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with overeating because it soothes and calms the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort that can lead to overindulgence. The saponins in marshmallows have a gentle anti-inflammatory effect, easing digestion and eliminating bloating, while also providing a sense of fullness and satisfaction. This natural remedy helps regulate appetite and portion control, allowing individuals to better manage their eating habits and achieve a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_properties: Emollient and soothing properties: Marshmallow decoction helps to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with eating large meals or irregular eating habits.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants in marshmallow root help protect the digestive lining from damage caused by overeating and promote healing of any inflammation or irritation that may occur.

Mucilages to bind and absorb: Marshmallow root's mucilage properties create a gel-like substance that can bind to and absorb excess fat and toxins, helping to regulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of overindulgence.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages in Althaea officinalis decoctions help with overeating by soothing the digestive tract, reducing inflammation, and promoting a sense of fullness and satiety.
Gallic acid: Gallic acid, a phenolic compound found in Althaea officinalis, may aid in reducing overeating by inhibiting the absorption of dietary fats and promoting weight loss.

Althaea flavonoids: Althaea flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, may help with overeating by reducing inflammation and modulating gut microbiota, leading to improved glucose metabolism and reduced appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are commonly used as they contain high concentrations of mucilage, a soothing and demulcent substance that can help alleviate digestive issues.
Stems: The stems of Althaea officinalis are also widely used for making decoctions, as they are rich in mucilage, which can help calm and protect the digestive tract.
Leaves: The leaves of Althaea officinalis are sometimes used for decoctions, containing mucilage and other compounds that can help soothe and regulate digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of althaea officinalis root and clean it thoroughly with cold running water.
Dry the root in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 5 grams of the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powder with 1 liter of boiling water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the residue.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with overeating because it aids digestion, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with consuming large meals. The menthol in peppermint helps to stimulate saliva production, breaking down food more efficiently and preventing the buildup of gas in the stomach. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for overeating. By soothing the digestive system and promoting relaxation, herbal peppermint decoctions can support healthy eating habits and alleviate symptoms of overindulgence.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant: Peppermint's decoction stimulates digestion, reducing symptoms of indigestion and bloating that can lead to overeating by promoting efficient nutrient absorption.
Carminative: The menthol content in peppermint decoction helps to expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and cramping that may trigger overeating as a coping mechanism.
Appetite suppressant: Peppermint's cooling effect can help reduce cravings for sweet or starchy foods, which are often associated with emotional eating, thereby supporting a healthier appetite.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It acts as a peripheral vasodilator and anesthetic, helping to reduce hunger and improve digestion.
Menthone: It possesses a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, potentially reducing excessive eating and alleviating symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
Rosmarinic acid (phenolic diterpene): It has been shown to have appetite-suppressing properties, likely due to its ability to reduce the release of ghrelin, a hormone that stimulates appetite.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used for decoctions related to overeating due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to alleviate digestive discomfort and nausea.
Stems: Stems are used in decoctions for overeating as they contain menthol and other compounds that help to calm the stomach and relieve digestive issues.
Roots: Roots of Mentha x piperita are also used in decoctions for overeating, as they contain a higher concentration of menthone, which helps to reduce nausea and alleviate digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon of dried m x piperita leaves into a clean glass container.
Combine the measured m x piperita leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the plant's properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container to remove the solids.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon decoctions helps with overeating because it stimulates digestion and regulates blood sugar levels. The active compounds in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde, help to slow down gastric emptying, keeping you feeling fuller for longer. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce insulin resistance, making it easier to manage cravings and portion sizes. As a natural appetite suppressant, Ceylon cinnamon decoctions promote a sense of fullness and satisfaction, ultimately helping individuals develop healthier eating habits.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ceylon cinnamon decoctions contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to cravings for unhealthy foods and overeating.

Alpha-hydroxyisobutylrate (AHIB) content: AHIB is a unique compound found in Ceylon cinnamon that has been shown to regulate blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity, making it more difficult to overindulge in sweets and carbs.

Cinnamaldehyde content: The active compound cinnamaldehyde in Ceylon cinnamon decoctions has been shown to reduce hunger and increase feelings of fullness by slowing gastric emptying and reducing the absorption of glucose into the bloodstream, making it easier to stick to a healthy diet.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: Acts as a natural appetite suppressant by reducing food cravings and improving digestion, making it easier to manage overeating.
Eugenol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate digestive discomfort associated with overeating, promoting a healthier relationship with food.
Cinnamomin: May aid in regulating blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity, which can reduce cravings for high-carbohydrate or high-sugar foods that often contribute to overeating.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Cinnamon rhyzomes are commonly used to make decoctions for overeating due to their ability to aid digestion and reduce bloating.
Barks: Cinnamon barks are used to make decoctions for overeating as they have a warming effect on the digestive system, helping to ease indigestion and discomfort.
Leaves: Cinnamon leaves are used to make decoctions for overeating due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of cinnamomum verum bark from mature trees with thick brown outer layers.
Clean the harvested bark by washing it with warm water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned cinnamomum verum bark in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 days or until it reaches 10% moisture content.
Grind 1-2 teaspoons of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the ground cinnamomum verum powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create the decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with overeating because it stimulates digestion, reducing feelings of bloating and discomfort that often accompany excessive food consumption. The warming properties of ginger also increase saliva production, breaking down carbohydrates more efficiently, which can help reduce cravings for unhealthy snacks. Additionally, the natural anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger may help regulate appetite hormones, promoting a sense of fullness and satisfaction, making it easier to stick to a balanced diet.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ginger decoctions stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, helping to alleviate bloating, discomfort, and indigestion associated with overeating.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory: Ginger's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive food intake, potentially reducing the risk of chronic diseases like diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease.

Carminative and Flatulence Reliever: Ginger decoctions have carminative properties that can help relieve bloating, gas, and flatulence often experienced after overindulging in food, making it easier to manage uncomfortable digestive symptoms.
remedy_constituents: 6-Gingerol: This compound helps with overeating by reducing inflammation and improving digestion, which in turn reduces the discomfort and bloating associated with overeating.

Gingerols: The gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol, 8-gingerol, and 10-gingerol, have been shown to slow down gastric emptying, reducing the rate at which food is digested and absorbed, and helping to prevent overeating.
Shogaols: Shogaols have been found to have a satiety-inducing effect, reducing appetite and food intake by activating certain receptors in the brain that signal fullness and satisfaction.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for decoctions as they contain the highest concentration of gingerols and shogaols, compounds known for their digestive benefits.
Roots: Roots are another commonly used part, as they contain similar compounds to rhyzomes and are often used in traditional medicine to aid digestion and relieve nausea.
Buds: Buds, which are the immature rhyzomes, are sometimes used in decoctions to help stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating associated with overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-10 fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with a sharp knife or trowel for medicinal use.
Wash the harvested rhizomes gently under running water to remove any dirt or debris thoroughly.
Slice the washed zingiber officinale rhizomes into thin pieces about 1-2 inches long.
Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan and add 1-2 teaspoons of sliced zingiber officinale pieces.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes then strain it with a fine-mesh sieve to create a decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava decoctions helps with overeating because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress-induced eating. The herb's anxiolytic properties also promote relaxation, making it easier to feel full and satisfied after meals. Additionally, kava's natural appetite suppressant properties may help regulate hunger hormones, leading to a reduction in overeating. As a result, incorporating herbal kava decoctions into one's routine can be a effective way to develop healthier eating habits and maintain a balanced diet.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kavalactones, a group of compounds found in kava, have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which can contribute to overeating behaviors. By promoting feelings of calmness and relaxation, kavalactones may help individuals make healthier food choices.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in kava have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help regulate appetite and metabolism by reducing inflammation that can lead to cravings for unhealthy foods. This property can contribute to a sense of fullness and satisfaction, making it easier to manage overeating.

Acylated Kavalactones: Acylated kavalactones have been found to have a satiating effect on the body, helping to reduce food intake by slowing down digestion and increasing feelings of fullness. This property can be particularly beneficial for individuals struggling with emotional eating or bingeing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are considered the most potent and effective part of the kava plant, as they contain a higher concentration of active compounds that help alleviate digestive discomfort and bloating caused by overeating.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the kava plant have been traditionally used to treat indigestion and other stomach ailments associated with overeating, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and soothe the gut.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried kava root in a clean glass jar for easy access.
Measure 10-20 grams of the dried root and transfer it to a stainless steel pot.
Add 1 liter of purified water to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid reaches desired consistency.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle decoctions helps with overeating because they work to balance and regulate hormones, particularly insulin and cortisol, which play a significant role in appetite control. Nettle's natural diuretic properties also help reduce fluid retention, leading to a decrease in perceived hunger and satiety. Additionally, nettle's anti-inflammatory effects soothe the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of bloating and discomfort often associated with overeating. By addressing these underlying hormonal and physiological imbalances, stinging nettle decoctions can support healthier eating habits and weight management.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Stinging nettle's diuretic effects can help reduce water retention, which can contribute to feelings of fullness and bloating, making it easier to manage portion sizes.
Digestive stimulant properties: Nettle decoctions have been shown to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, reducing symptoms of indigestion and discomfort that may lead to overeating.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is associated with chronic conditions like obesity and metabolic disorders, making it easier to manage cravings and make healthier food choices.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in fiber, vitamins, and minerals that help to regulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of overeating.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe digestive issues caused by overeating.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried urtica dioica leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herb mixture for 10-15 minutes in a heat-resistant container.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Discard the solids and allow the decoction to cool to room temperature within 30 minutes.
Store the cooled decoction in an amber glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with overeating because it stimulates digestion and improves appetite regulation. The aromatic compounds in rosemary, such as camphor and borneol, increase saliva production and enhance the breakdown of fats and proteins, reducing bloating and discomfort after meals. Additionally, rosemary's ability to improve blood circulation may help reduce cravings by increasing feelings of fullness and satisfaction, making it a natural remedy for those struggling with overindulgence.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Rosemary decoction stimulates digestive enzymes and improves digestion, which can help reduce bloating and discomfort caused by overeating, making it easier to feel full and satisfied.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory: Rosemary's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to emotional eating and overeating behaviors.

Cognitive Enhancer and Mood Regulator: Rosemary has been shown to improve cognitive function, including attention and memory, and can also have a calming effect on the mind and emotions, potentially reducing impulse snacking and overeating caused by emotional triggers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are used to make decoctions as they have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating caused by overeating.
Barks: Rosemary barks contain compounds that help stimulate digestion and relieve stomach cramps, making them an effective part for reducing discomfort after overindulging.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems for the decoction.
Rinse the harvested herbs with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris then chop them coarsely.
Combine the chopped herbs with 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Steep the herb mixture in hot water for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse and dissolve.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids then store it in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen decoctions helps with overeating because it targets the digestive system, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy gut. The menthol content in wintergreen has a calming effect on the stomach, alleviating feelings of discomfort and bloating associated with overindulgence. Additionally, wintergreen's natural properties help to stimulate digestion, allowing for proper nutrient absorption and waste elimination, which can reduce cravings for unhealthy foods. This holistic approach helps regulate appetite and support sustainable weight management.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol, one of the main active compounds in wintergreen, has natural appetite-suppressing effects by stimulating digestion and increasing saliva production, which helps reduce hunger pangs.
Salicylic acid: Wintergreen's salicylic acid content has been shown to inhibit the breakdown of fats in the body, reducing cravings for high-calorie foods and helping with weight management.

Mucilage: The mucilaginous properties of wintergreen help soothe and calm digestive discomforts, such as bloating and indigestion, associated with overeating, promoting a sense of fullness and satisfaction.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicylic acid, wintergreen roots help alleviate digestive discomfort and bloating caused by overeating.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of methyl salicylate, rhyzomes work as a natural anti-inflammatory to soothe stomach upset and indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems according to traditional herbal practices.
Rinse the harvested material gently with cool water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems with 250 milliliters of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes before straining through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Discard solids and store the decoction in an amber glass bottle refrigerated for up to five days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cassia decoctions helps with overeating because it stimulates digestion, alleviates bloating, and regulates blood sugar levels. The saponins present in cassia seeds help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to feelings of fullness and satiety. Carminative property: The carminative properties of cassia decoction help to relieve flatulence, cramps, and discomfort in the abdomen, reducing feelings of fullness and discomfort that can lead to overeating.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a popular choice for making decoctions as they have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, helping to alleviate symptoms of overeating.
Barks: The barks of the cassia plant contain compounds that have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation, which can occur as a result of overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of senna auriculata measuring one ounce per quart of water.
Cleanse and chop the herbs into small pieces for efficient release of bioactive compounds.
Combine the chopped herbs with four cups of boiling water in a large glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes allowing the active ingredients to infuse fully.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve discarding solids and store.

remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried rauvolfia serpentina root, 2 cups of water, and a clean glass jar with lid.
Grind the dried rauvolfia serpentina root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground rauvolfia serpentina powder with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot, steeping for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing in refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Take 2 tablespoons of the prepared rauvolfia serpentina decoction three times a day, 30 minutes before meals, to help manage overeating symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of dried achyranthes aspera root powder and two cups of purified water.
Combine the root powder and water in a saucepan and bring it to a boil on high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the mixture has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to three days and consume one cup twice daily after meals.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves of recommended variety for digestive purposes weighing approximately 100-200 grams.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with warm water and gentle soap to remove dirt debris and bacteria.
Chop the cleaned aloe vera leaves into small pieces of about 1-2 centimeters in length to facilitate extraction of active compounds.
Steep the chopped aloe vera pieces in boiling distilled water for 15-20 minutes to create a decoction with optimal nutrient content.
Strain the cooled decoction through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove solids and sediment before storing in an airtight container.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried turmeric rhizomes from the plant curcuma longa.
Grind the dried turmeric into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Boil 500ml of water in a saucepan for 5 minutes to sterilize it before use.
Add one teaspoon of ground turmeric to the boiling water and reduce heat for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle after cooling slightly.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried ulmus rubra bark and soak it in 4 cups of boiling water.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Mix the strained decoction with 1 tablespoon of honey to mask any bitter flavors and improve palatability.
Allow the mixture to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days for consumption.

remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and one cup of boiling water.
Combine the ingredients in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup or teapot for infusion.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes before straining it using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature or store it in an airtight container in refrigerator.
Consume one quarter cup of the cooled decoction three times daily as needed.

remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality melissa officinalis dried leaves and stems and measure out 2 teaspoons for each cup of water used.
Combine the measured melissa officinalis with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then carefully pour the decoction into a teapot or cup without disturbing the solids.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids after 20-30 minutes of total preparation time.
Store the cooled and strained melissa officinalis decoction in an airtight glass bottle and consume within 24 hours for optimal benefits.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality echinacea angustifolia root material for future use.

Combine the powdered echinacea with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried red clover flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.

remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried arctium lappa root powder.
Boil two cups of water in a saucepan for five minutes.
Add the powdered root to the boiling water and stir well.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until liquid reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard solids.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried anethum graveolens herb per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured herb with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes depending on desired strength and flavor.

Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in a covered ceramic cup.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Add honey to taste and mix well before serving warm.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 15 grams of dried illicium verum roots and stems from a trusted supplier or local herb store.

Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large glass container, discarding solids.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overeating that are treated with herbal decoctions are digestive issues such as bloating, constipation, and indigestion. supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with overeating is a blend of peppermint, ginger, and dandelion root. Peppermint soothes digestive issues and calms the stomach, while ginger aids in digestion and reduces inflammation. Dandelion root supports liver function and promotes the elimination of toxins. This combination helps regulate appetite, reduces cravings for unhealthy foods, and promotes overall digestive health. It can be consumed as a tea or added to a warm bath for a relaxing and rejuvenating experience.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytotherapy Research: PTR, herbal decoctions have been found to be effective in managing obesity and metabolic syndrome. The study, which included 279 clinical trials, found that herbs containing green tea, Phaseolus vulgaris, Garcinia cambogia, and Nigella sativa, among others, significantly reduced body mass index, waist circumference, and total cholesterol levels. Specifically, the intake of green tea resulted in a significant improvement in weight, body mass index, waist circumference, hip circumference, and total cholesterol levels, indicating that herbal decoctions can be beneficial in addressing overeating and promoting weight management.
